metrics.name: BoJ
metrics.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

simulation_result.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.875 (counted n=3); simulation-conditional 0.0364 (resolved mass 1.00); headline 0.0364 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
bounded omitted-state residual 0.631: the interval widens to [0.0, 0.7153] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: The BOJ Policy Board votes on a proposal to raise the uncollateralized overnight call rate from 0.75% to 1.0%. A simple majority of the 9 members is required. The outcome hinges on whether the three April dissenters (Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura) are joined by at least two more members, or whether Gover

terminal_canonicalization.why: vote terminals read the tally directly

boj_gradual_hiking_cycle.claim: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.

provenance.definition: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: BOJ raised policy rate from -0.1% to 0-0.1% in March 2024, then to 0.25% in July 2024, then to 0.5% in January 2025, then to 0.75% in March 2025.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

dissenting_pressure_for_hike.claim: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.

provenance.definition: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, three members proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but were defeated 6-3.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

market_expectations_elevated.claim: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.

provenance.definition: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Reuters reported that the BOJ has 'locked in' signals for a June rate hike, and nearly two-thirds of economists expect 1.0% by end-June.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan has locked in signals for a June rate hike, with nearly two-thirds of economists expecting the rate to reach 1.0% by end-June.

nakagawa_junko.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Nakagawa Junko votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Nakagawa Junko dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where she supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Nakagawa Junko proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

takata_hajime.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Takata Hajime votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Takata Hajime dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where he supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Takata Hajime proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

tamura_naoki.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Tamura Naoki votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Tamura Naoki dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where he supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Tamura Naoki proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

ueda_kazuo.quantity: this governor votes with the majority on rate decisions

provenance.definition: Governor Ueda votes in favor of the final decision, whether to hold or raise.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Ueda supports the majority decision.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Governor Ueda voted with the majority to keep the rate unchanged at 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Policy Board voted 6-3 to keep the rate unchanged.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting

provenance.definition: The BOJ announces a guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate higher than the current 0.75%.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any increase in the rate, e.g., to 1.0% or higher.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the March 18-19, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.5% to 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

cases.description: At the January 23-24, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.25% to 0.5%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.5%.

cases.description: At the July 30-31, 2024 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0-0.1% to 0.25%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.25%.

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect the vote count; quorum must be met.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

other_members.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['maintain at 0.75%', 'raise to 1.0%'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda believes the economy needs more time to confirm the wage-price spiral is sustainable, and that a premature hike could destabilize markets or the yen carry trade unwind.
beliefs: Inflation expectations are anchored but fragile
Real wages need to turn positive for several months
Global financial conditions remain uncertain
commitments: Past pattern of cautious moves (e.g., March 2024 exit from negative rates, July 2024 hike, then pause)
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Government pressure to avoid excessive yen appreciation hurting exporters; market expectations for a hike are high but not overwhelming.

relationships.other_members: mixed, but majority sided with him in April

stances: Prefers to wait for more data
supporting_evidence_ids: Oxford Economics Nagai argument on insufficient data
historical_case_refs: BOJ July 2024 hike followed by long pause
Ueda's cautious approach since April 2023
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not explicitly pre-committed to June
He emphasized data dependence in post-April press conference
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote to hold the rate at 0.75%, arguing that more evidence on wage growth and consumption is needed.
assumptions: Inflation data for May will be mixed

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda is privately convinced a hike is needed to maintain credibility, but he wants to appear as if he is bowing to board pressure rather than leading the move.
beliefs: Inflation is above target sustainably
Waiting risks a sharper adjustment later
Board unity is important
goals: Preserve his reputation as a consensus-builder
Avoid being seen as weak on inflation
commitments: Past willingness to compromise (e.g., allowed dissents in April)
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Strong market expectations; three dissents already on record; media narrative of 'locked-in' hike.

relationships.other_members: some may swing if he signals support

historical_case_refs: BOJ July 2024 hike where Ueda led but allowed dissents
Ueda's shift from dovish to hawkish in mid-2024
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not publicly ruled out June
He allowed the three dissents to be recorded, signaling openness
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote for a 25bp hike to 1.0%, framing it as a response to accumulating evidence and board consensus.
assumptions: May inflation data will be firm

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa is convinced that inflation is entrenched and that the BOJ must act decisively to prevent overheating and asset bubbles.
beliefs: Inflation expectations are de-anchoring upward
Labor market is tight
Delaying hikes will require sharper tightening later
goals: Normalize rates to neutral as soon as possible
Prevent yen weakness from becoming disorderly
commitments: Consistent hawkish votes in past meetings
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations; her own public statements; academic background favoring preemptive action.
historical_case_refs: Her consistent hawkish stance since joining board
Similar dissents by other BOJ hawks in 2000s
distinguishing_observations: She has been the most vocal hawk
She often cites inflation expectations surveys
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Propose a 25bp hike to 1.0% and argue forcefully for it, possibly even proposing a larger hike if she thinks it's warranted.
assumptions: Inflation data will support her view

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that global financial conditions are deteriorating rapidly due to a sudden spike in US long-term yields and a sharp yen depreciation, which could destabilize Japanese import prices and inflation expectations. She sees the 0.75% rate as insufficient to anchor expecta
beliefs: Global bond market stress is transmitting to Japan
Yen depreciation is fueling import inflation beyond target
A rate above 0.75% is needed to preempt second-round effects
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are elevated; dissenting pressure from allies Takata and Tamura; Ueda tries to persuade her to wait for more data.
historical_case_refs: BOJ July 2024 rate hike to 0.25% amid yen weakness
ECB 2011 rate hike reversal due to sovereign debt crisis
distinguishing_observations: She voted for 1.0% in April 2026 but might accept a compromise
She has not publicly cited global risks before
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Nakagawa Junko would propose a rate increase to 0.85% or 0.90% as a compromise, arguing that a modest hike addresses inflation risks while acknowledging global uncertainty. She would vote against main

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that the Japanese government is exerting strong behind-the-scenes pressure on the BOJ to avoid a rate hike above 0.75% due to upcoming elections and the government's high debt servicing costs. She fears that raising rates could trigger a bond market selloff and fisc
beliefs: Government is opposed to a hike above 0.75%
Fiscal sustainability concerns are paramount
BOJ independence is being tested
goals: Secure a commitment to future normalization
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Ueda tries to persuade her to align with the government's wishes; Takata and Tamura push for a hike; other members are swayed by fiscal concerns.
historical_case_refs: BOJ 2000 rate hike reversal after political backlash
BOJ 2013-2016 QQE under government pressure
distinguishing_observations: She has not previously cited political pressure in her dissents
Her April 2026 vote for 1.0% suggests strong conviction
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Nakagawa Junko would vote to maintain the rate at 0.75% but issue a strongly worded statement emphasizing that future hikes are likely if economic conditions permit. She would privately seek a written

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that the BOJ Policy Board is deeply divided and that her previous dissenting votes have isolated her from the majority. She fears that continuing to dissent could undermine her influence on future policy decisions. Her private reality is that she may vote with the m
beliefs: Board cohesion is necessary for effective policy
Her dissents are losing impact
Ueda may offer concessions for her vote
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Ueda tries to persuade her to join the majority; Takata and Tamura expect her to dissent; other members view her as obstructionist.
historical_case_refs: BOJ 2007-2008 board divisions during rate hikes
Fed 2015-2016 dissents and later consensus building
distinguishing_observations: She has dissented three times in a row
Her April 2026 vote was a clear minority
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Nakagawa Junko would vote to maintain the rate at 0.75% but issue a statement indicating that she expects a rate hike at the next meeting. She would privately negotiate with Ueda for a faster pace of

takata_hajime.claim: Takata is hawkish but genuinely data-dependent; he will vote for a hike only if incoming data clearly supports it, otherwise he may hold.
beliefs: Inflation is above target but may be transitory
Wage growth needs to be confirmed
Global slowdown risks are real
commitments: But has shown flexibility in past votes
takata_hajime.pressures: Market expectations; his own analysis of global economy.
supporting_evidence_ids: Oxford Economics Nagai argument on insufficient data
historical_case_refs: His voting record shows occasional holds when data is mixed
distinguishing_observations: He often emphasizes 'data-dependent' in speeches
He has not pre-committed to June
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote for a hike only if May CPI and wage data are strong; otherwise, vote to hold and wait for July.
assumptions: May data will be available before the meeting

takata_hajime.claim: Takata believes that the current gradual hiking cycle is too slow to prevent inflation from overshooting the 2% target, and that a decisive hike to above 0.75% is necessary now to anchor expectations and avoid a future sharp tightening.
beliefs: Inflation persistence is higher than the majority estimates
Waiting risks a wage-price spiral that would require even more painful tightening later
goals: Maintain BOJ credibility as an inflation fighter
takata_hajime.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are elevated, and delaying could trigger yen depreciation and imported inflation.
stances: Strongly hawkish on near-term rate action
supporting_evidence_ids: Three dissenting members proposed 1.0% in April
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 1989-1990 tightening delay that led to asset bubble burst
ECB's 2022-2023 experience with persistent inflation
distinguishing_observations: Takata voted for 1.0% in April despite majority opposition
He has not publicly backed down from that stance
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote for a hike to 1.0% or higher, and argue forcefully for a faster pace of normalization.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata is privately alarmed by global financial instability (e.g., US recession fears, European banking stress) and believes that hiking above 0.75% could trigger a domestic credit crunch or capital flight, so he now prioritizes financial stability over inflation control.
beliefs: Global risks have materially increased since April
A rate hike now could be pro-cyclical and destabilizing
goals: Preserve BOJ's ability to act as a backstop
takata_hajime.pressures: Dissenting pressure for hike from markets and some colleagues, but his own risk assessment has shifted.
stances: Willing to pause even if inflation remains above target
supporting_evidence_ids: Market expectations elevated (but could be wrong)
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2000 rate hike reversal after dot-com bust
Fed's 2019 mid-cycle pivot due to trade war fears
distinguishing_observations: Takata listens to arguments from other members
He respects Ueda's caution
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote to hold at 0.75% or support a smaller hike (e.g., to 0.85%) with a dovish statement emphasizing global risks.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata sees the 0.75% threshold as a tactical bargaining chip: he publicly demands above 0.75% to extract concessions on forward guidance or quantitative tightening, but privately is willing to accept a hold if the majority offers a stronger hawkish signal on future hikes.
beliefs: The majority can be moved on communication even if not on the rate
A symbolic win on language is more valuable than a rate hike now
goals: Shift the BOJ's forward guidance to a more hawkish bias
Avoid being isolated as a perpetual dissenter
takata_hajime.pressures: Institutional incentive to maintain majority cohesion; personal desire to influence policy beyond just voting.
stances: Willing to trade rate action for policy signal
supporting_evidence_ids: He listens to arguments from other members
Nakagawa is an ally but not as aggressive
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2016 comprehensive assessment where dissenters shaped forward guidance
Fed's 2015-2016 'dot plot' negotiations
distinguishing_observations: Takata is a policy board member, not a single-issue activist
He has relationships with both hawks and doves
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote for a hold at 0.75% but insist on a statement that explicitly signals a high probability of a hike at the next meeting, or a reduction in bond purchases.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura believes the BOJ has fallen behind the curve and that a series of hikes is needed to restore credibility, regardless of short-term data fluctuations.
beliefs: Inflation is structurally above target due to labor shortages
BOJ must act preemptively
Waiting risks a crisis
goals: Raise rates to at least 1.5% by year-end
tamura_naoki.pressures: His own conviction; market expectations; academic reputation.
historical_case_refs: His past dissents for larger moves
Similar to hawkish BOJ members in 1989-90
distinguishing_observations: He has publicly argued that 0.75% is too low
He often cites structural factors
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Propose a 25bp hike to 1.0%, and possibly argue for a larger move if he thinks it's justified.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura Naoki privately believes that global financial instability—particularly from emerging market debt distress and a sharp yen carry trade unwind—poses an imminent systemic risk to Japan's financial system, making a rate hike too dangerous at this meeting.
beliefs: A rate hike above 0.75% would accelerate yen carry trade unwinding, triggering a sudden capital outflow and asset price collapse.
The BOJ's gradual hiking cycle must pause to monitor global contagion effects from recent emerging market defaults.
The dissenting pressure for a hike is driven by short-term inflation concerns, ignoring the bigger picture of financial stability.
goals: Prevent a financial crisis by keeping rates unchanged at 0.75%.
Advocate for enhanced macroprudential measures instead of a rate hike.
Maintain Japan's financial system resilience during global turbulence.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Intense market expectations for a hike conflict with his growing fear of systemic risk; he faces pressure from global financial institutions and domestic banks warning of carry trade instability.
supporting_evidence_ids: As of May 13, 2026, the BOJ maintained the rate at 0.75% with a 6-3 vote, indicating deep division.
The April 28 statement noted three dissenting members proposed 1.0%, but Tamura was among them—now he has reversed his stance.
contradicting_evidence_ids: He voted for a hike in April, so a reversal requires a major new event.
Market expectations are elevated for a hike, and his previous public stance was hawkish.
historical_case_refs: 1997 Asian Financial Crisis: BOJ kept rates low despite pressure, later praised for stability.
2008 Global Financial Crisis: BOJ cut rates after initial hikes, showing risk of premature tightening.
distinguishing_observations: Tamura has recently met with Bank of Japan financial stability officials more frequently.
He has made private remarks about 'storm clouds on the horizon' in internal memos.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura would vote to keep the rate unchanged at 0.75% and deliver a strong statement emphasizing global risks, possibly joining with Ueda to form a majority for no hike.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura Naoki privately believes that the BOJ's credibility is at stake if it does not follow through on its forward guidance and market expectations; he must vote for a hike to 1.0% to demonstrate the Board's commitment to normalizing policy and to prevent yen depreciation from fueling imported infl
beliefs: The BOJ's gradual hiking cycle must be seen as credible; pausing now would signal weakness and trigger yen depreciation.
Market expectations are elevated for a hike; failing to deliver would cause a loss of confidence and financial instability.
The three dissenting votes in April show strong internal support for a hike, and he must lead that faction.
goals: Raise the rate to 1.0% to anchor inflation expectations and support the yen.
Demonstrate the BOJ's independence and commitment to data-dependent normalization.
Prevent a wage-price spiral by acting preemptively.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Strong market expectations for a hike, political pressure from export industries suffering from a weak yen, and internal pressure from fellow hawks to deliver a decisive move.
supporting_evidence_ids: He voted for a hike to 1.0% in April, showing consistent hawkishness.
Market expectations are elevated for a hike, as noted in shared conditions.
Dissenting pressure for a hike is high, and he is a leader of that faction.
contradicting_evidence_ids: The majority voted to hold in April, suggesting the Board is cautious.
Global risks could argue against a hike, but he dismisses them as manageable.
historical_case_refs: 2000 BOJ rate hike: Ended zero rates, later reversed, but showed commitment to normalization.
2022-2023 Fed hikes: Powell's credibility was built on following through on guidance despite market turmoil.
distinguishing_observations: Tamura has given speeches emphasizing the need to 'normalize policy without delay'.
He has privately told allies that 'the window for a hike is closing'.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura would vote to raise the rate to 1.0% and actively lobby other members, possibly securing a 5-4 or 6-3 majority for a hike.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura Naoki privately believes that a compromise is necessary to maintain Board unity and avoid a damaging public split; he will propose a smaller hike to 0.85% or 0.90% as a middle ground, signaling a continued tightening cycle without shocking markets or alienating the cautious majority.
beliefs: A full hike to 1.0% would be too aggressive and risk a market overreaction, but keeping rates at 0.75% would be seen as dovish and weaken the yen.
A modest hike of 10-15 basis points would satisfy market expectations for normalization while preserving flexibility.
Board unity is critical for the BOJ's credibility; a split vote would undermine the message.
goals: Achieve a rate increase that is seen as a credible step in the hiking cycle.
Preserve Board cohesion and avoid a repeat of the 6-3 split.
Signal that the BOJ is data-dependent but committed to gradual tightening.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are high, but so are concerns about global risks; he must navigate between hawks and doves to find a consensus.
supporting_evidence_ids: The April vote was 6-3, showing a deep split that could be bridged by a compromise.
Market expectations are elevated, but not necessarily for a full 25bp hike—some analysts expect a smaller move.
contradicting_evidence_ids: He voted for 1.0% in April, so a smaller hike might be seen as a retreat.
Some hawks may reject a compromise as too timid.
historical_case_refs: 2006 BOJ rate hike: The Board compromised on a 25bp hike after earlier divisions.
2013 Fed taper tantrum: Bernanke used gradual steps to avoid shocking markets.
distinguishing_observations: Tamura has recently been seen in private meetings with both Ueda and Nakagawa, suggesting bridge-building.
He has used the phrase 'measured steps' in recent internal communications.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura would propose a 10-15bp hike and vote for it, urging both hawks and doves to support the compromise. He would likely secure a majority, possibly 7-2 or 8-1.

other_members.claim: The remaining six members (excluding the three hawks) are broadly aligned with Ueda's cautious approach and will vote to hold unless Ueda signals a change.
beliefs: Inflation is moderating
Wage growth is uncertain
Global risks are elevated
other_members.pressures: Market expectations but also government preference for stability.
historical_case_refs: BOJ board typically follows governor
Similar dynamics in 2024
distinguishing_observations: No public statements indicating a shift
They have not signaled a change since April
other_members.action_if_state: Vote to hold the rate at 0.75%, following Ueda's recommendation.

other_members.claim: Some of the six members have become convinced by the hawks' arguments or by market pressure, and are now leaning toward a hike, potentially swinging the vote.
beliefs: Inflation is sticky
Waiting could be costly
Market expectations matter
commitments: Previously voted hold but are open to change
other_members.pressures: Market expectations at 63%; media narrative; three dissents in April.
stances: Will decide based on May data
historical_case_refs: BOJ board shifts in 2006 when majority swung to hike
Similar swing in July 2024
distinguishing_observations: No public statements yet, but private signals may have changed
Some members have expressed concern about yen weakness
other_members.action_if_state: Vote for a 25bp hike to 1.0%, providing the majority needed to pass the proposal.
assumptions: Ueda may also swing or allow the vote

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect the vote count; quorum must be met.

checks.note: all terminal-writing effects are known mechanical kinds
at least one resolving path exists
vote tally in members — exact units
synthetic YES→1 / NO→0 through the live path

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: The government's exact tolerance for a hike and the strength of fiscal support are unclear.
deliberations.note: The government's opposition to a hike above 0.75% is clear, but fiscal sustainability concerns are paramount. Ueda's pressure to align with government wishes tests BOJ independence. Takata and Tamura'

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the May data is strong enough to justify a hike now, or if we need more time to confirm the trend.
deliberations.note: The May data is not yet definitive, but market expectations at 63% and the risk of losing credibility if we wait too long push me toward action. The three dissents in April show division, but acting n

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the recent inflation is truly transitory or becoming entrenched, and whether wage growth is broad enough to sustain the 2% targ
deliberations.note: I weigh the risk of acting prematurely against the risk of falling behind the curve. The April CPI data showed some moderation, but services inflation remains sticky. Wage negotiations yielded 5%+ inc

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the recent improvement in nominal wages will translate into sustained positive real wage growth.
deliberations.note: The recent nominal wage data shows improvement, but real wages have not yet turned positive for a sustained period. Prematurely hiking could shock markets and undermine the fragile inflation expectati

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether wage growth will sustain enough to support a rate hike without harming the economy.
deliberations.note: Weighing the tradeoff: maintaining the rate at 0.75% aligns with stability and following Ueda's lead, but the unresolved issue of wage growth sustainability creates uncertainty. A hike could harm the

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: I am uncertain whether other board members fully grasp the systemic risk from a rate hike at this juncture.
deliberations.note: I must weigh the market's intense expectation for a hike against my deep fear that raising rates above 0.75% will trigger a yen carry trade unwinding, causing capital flight and asset collapse. The gl

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: The exact balance of views among other board members is unclear, though I know Ueda, Nakagawa, and Takata have stances. I need to gauge
deliberations.note: I initially voted to hold at 0.75% to avoid market overreaction and preserve unity, but I now realize that with market expectations high and Ueda, Nakagawa, and Takata likely favoring a hike, a hold c

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether other members are willing to accept a hawkish language change in exchange for maintaining the rate.
deliberations.note: I need to gauge whether other members will accept a hawkish language change in exchange for maintaining the rate. I recall that some members are uneasy about inflation persistence but wary of overtigh

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether global recession risks will materialize quickly enough to offset the need for a hike.
deliberations.note: I am weighing the risk of global recession against the immediate need to hike. The bond market stress and yen depreciation are already transmitting real inflation. Waiting risks de-anchoring expectati

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether Ueda will offer meaningful concessions for my vote.
deliberations.note: I need to weigh whether Ueda will offer concessions. He likely values Board cohesion and may offer a faster tightening schedule in exchange for my vote, but if I dissent, I risk further marginalizatio

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the three dissents will shift if I signal support for a hike, or if maintaining rate would fracture the board further.
deliberations.note: The three dissents signal deep board division, and my hawkish lean risks fracturing unity further if I push a hike without broader support. However, waiting could be seen as weakness on inflation, dam

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the government will accept a hold or retaliate against the BOJ.
deliberations.note: The government's fiscal concerns are real, but a hold at 0.75% avoids immediate confrontation while preserving room for future normalization. Ueda's persuasion suggests he may accept a hold if it's fr

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether recent inflation is truly transitory or becoming persistent, and whether wage growth will broaden sufficiently.
deliberations.note: I've weighed the tradeoff between acting now to signal inflation vigilance versus waiting for clearer data. Market expectations for a hold are strong, and global slowdown risks are real. My allies Nak

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the recent improvement in real wages is durable and broad-based.
deliberations.note: The recent improvement in real wages is promising but not yet broad-based or durable. A premature hike could strengthen the yen, hurt exporters, and destabilize inflation expectations. The majority si

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the majority is truly movable on communication if I signal willingness to support the rate hold.
deliberations.note: I've weighed the tradeoff: supporting a rate hold in exchange for a hawkish shift in forward guidance. The majority is likely movable on communication if I signal willingness to hold, as they value co

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the swing members will support a hike if I signal strong leadership.
deliberations.note: The board is deeply divided with three dissents already on record. If I signal strong leadership for a hike now, swing members may resist, fracturing unity and damaging my reputation as a consensus-bu

coalescer.randomness_rule: content-addressed (state/context-seeded) draws — equal keys share the future transition law by construction; independent streams carry a tag that refuses the merge

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine); represented states carry the full branch mass — omitted-state residuals are bounded interval-wideners, never unknown-state worlds

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: behavioral replicates not requested (deterministic execution: content-addressed draws make replicate 0 exhaustive)

provenance.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting
provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

consequences.boundary_contract: delivery mechanics precompiled; interpretation/persuasion/reaction remain branch-local actor decisions

vote_on_rate.description: Each Policy Board member casts vote on proposed rate.
